items.caption: A diagram illustrating the structure of a plant cell wall. the cell wall is composed of various layers, including the chloroplast, cell membrane, primary cell wall, and secondary cell wall. the chloroplast is the green organelle where photosynthesis occurs, and the cell membrane is the outer boundary of the cell. the primary cell wall is composed of cellulose, pectin, and lignin, and is flexible and thin. the secondary cell wall is stiff and lignified, providing support and water resistance. the image also contains text that reads "all plant cells are contained by a cell wall."
